label: About
title: About Ceramic Aperture Atlas
short_blurb: A daily generated front page where the image is the interface.
body: Daily Frontpage is a daily generated interactive front page. Each edition starts from recent saved signals and source research, becomes a new image-led scene, and stays explorable in the archive. The image is the interface: visible marks, objects, and surfaces in the scene open the real sources that shaped the day.

This iteration began with 74 recent saved-signal files and 78 captured links, then narrowed the page to 9 source windows around In Guadalajara, a Ceramic Tile Mural Mimics a Sun-Drenched Ecosystem and @jonaasw1: cool internet things pt. 9 - digital MoMA gallery - personal "digital home", plus 7 more. A source image from thisiscolossal.com helped set the palette, contrast, edge behavior, and gesture. The scene became Ceramic Aperture Atlas: Overhead scan of a textile field made from sun-washed ceramic tiles: irregular rectangles, matte grout seams, and broad organic color islands structure the whole plate. After the image was made, a plate-reading pass mapped 9 visible regions and the mask audit tightened them against large cream ceramic tile field, lavender tile island at upper left, and cyan tile coast at right edge.

typography.rationale: Bricolage Grotesque and Space Grotesk fit record, arcade, and listening-room editions while Fraunces keeps source captions tactile.
